text.title: Lookalike and Retargeting Audience Design Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Lookalike and Retargeting Audience Design. Each pairs symptom + sourced root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Acquisition campaign serves existing customers; 25% of impressions waste.
antipattern.root-cause: exclusion-stack-mandatory rule violated; quick-setup omitted exclusion.
antipattern.fix: Build standing exclusion list (customers + opt-outs + 90-day buyers); attach to every acquisition campaign.

antipattern.symptom: Seed of 200 purchase events produces a 'lookalike' that performs worse than broad targeting.
antipattern.fix: Combine seed with longer time window or higher-funnel events until >= 1000; or use interest-targeting until seed grows.

antipattern.symptom: Same retargeted user sees 25 impressions/week; CTR collapses 60% in 14 days.
antipattern.root-cause: frequency-cap-discipline rule violated; no cap configured.
antipattern.fix: Set 8/week cap at adset; rotate creatives weekly to delay fatigue.

antipattern.symptom: Three retargeting campaigns serve same users; CPM inflates 40%.
antipattern.fix: Run Meta Audience Insights overlap check; consolidate campaigns with >30% overlap.
